proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 33 candidate features, the continuous variable feature_015 (range 30-90, mean ~65) is positively associated with pfs_months: higher feature_015 corresponds to longer pfs_months.
The 3-level categorical feature_001 has a monotone negative association with pfs_months: mean pfs_months is highest at feature_001=0 and lowest at feature_001=2.
Binary feature_014 (prevalence ~55%) is associated with shorter pfs_months: patients with feature_014=1 have lower mean pfs_months than feature_014=0.

analyses.code:
from scipy.stats import pearsonr, spearmanr
pearsonr(df['feature_015'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=0.766 (p<1e-300), Spearman rho=0.785. Mean pfs_months by quintiles of feature_015: 1.86, 3.34, 4.30, 5.27, 6.83 — strong, monotone, positive.
Mean pfs_months: feature_001=0: 5.27 (n=17592), =1: 4.05 (n=24971), =2: 2.91 (n=7437). Monotone decreasing; per-level OLS coefficient -1.17 mo (p<1e-300).
Mean pfs_months 5.06 (feature_014=0) vs 3.70 (feature_014=1); difference -1.35 mo, t-test p<1e-300.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Binary feature_027 (prevalence ~20%) is positively associated with pfs_months — patients with feature_027=1 have longer pfs_months than feature_027=0; this positive direction is unusual relative to the other significant binaries and suggests feature_027 may be a treatment.
Continuous feature_005 (right-skewed, mean ~9, max 777) is negatively associated with pfs_months.
Continuous feature_019 (mean ~3.8, std 0.5) is positively associated with pfs_months.
Continuous feature_025 (mean ~3.8, std 3.9) is negatively associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs_months 4.12 (feature_027=0) vs 5.09 (feature_027=1); difference +0.97 mo, t-test p<1e-300.
Pearson r=-0.0377, p=3.1e-17. Modest but significant negative association in raw scale.
Pearson r=+0.102, p=3e-115. Positive, highly significant.
Pearson r=-0.123, p=2e-167.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Binary features feature_023 (~42%) and feature_006 (~35%) are each associated with shorter pfs_months as univariate main effects.
Rare binaries feature_022 (~4.5%) and feature_011 (~3%) have small but detectable main-effect associations with pfs_months: feature_022 negative, feature_011 positive.

analyses.result_summary: feature_023: mean pfs 4.45 (=0) vs 4.12 (=1), diff -0.33 mo, p=8.5e-56. feature_006: mean pfs 4.42 (=0) vs 4.11 (=1), diff -0.31 mo, p=5.2e-46.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After joint adjustment (multivariable OLS) the eight strongest predictors of pfs_months will be feature_015 (+), feature_001 (-), feature_014 (-), feature_027 (+), feature_025 (-), feature_019 (+), feature_023 (-), feature_006 (-), with feature_027 retaining a positive association of roughly +1 month.

analyses.result_summary: R^2=0.859. Adjusted coefficients (sign, t-statistic): feature_015 +0.176/yr (t=454), feature_001 -1.17/level (t=-206), feature_014 -1.36 (t=-176), feature_027 +0.94 (t=98), feature_025 -0.076/unit (t=-76), feature_019 +0.47/unit (t=61), feature_023 -0.35 (t=-44), feature_006 -0.29 (t=-36), feature_005 -0.005/unit (t=-21), feature_022 -0.36 (t=-19), feature_021 -0.0004/unit (t=-9). Remaining features have |t|<2.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_027 is best interpreted as a treatment indicator; under that interpretation, its average treatment effect on pfs_months (after adjustment for prognostic features) is approximately +1 month.

feature_011, feature_012, feature_018, feature_020, feature_024, feature_010 and feature_031 are not main-effect prognostic features in the joint model, so any of these could be additional treatments with heterogeneous effects.

analyses.code: OLS adjusting for feature_015, feature_001, feature_014, feature_019, feature_025, feature_005, feature_021
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted feature_027 coefficient = +0.95 mo (95% CI ~0.93 to 0.97), p<1e-300. Direction confirmed.

analyses.code: Multivariable OLS p-values for feature_010, feature_012, feature_018, feature_020, feature_024, feature_031 — all non-significant (|t|<2)
analyses.result_summary: None of these variables have a detectable main effect on pfs_months once feature_015, feature_001, feature_014 are controlled. They remain candidates for treatments only if their effects are heterogeneous.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The positive effect of feature_027 on pfs_months is roughly constant across levels of feature_001 (the 3-level severity-like variable), i.e. there is no feature_027 × feature_001 interaction.
The positive effect of feature_027 on pfs_months is roughly constant across feature_014, i.e. there is no feature_027 × feature_014 interaction.

analyses.result_summary: feature_027 effect within feature_001=0: +0.93 mo; within feature_001=1: +0.99 mo; within feature_001=2: +0.90 mo. No meaningful heterogeneity (interaction p=0.18 in OLS).
feature_027 effect within feature_014=0: +0.94 mo; within feature_014=1: +1.01 mo. Essentially uniform.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_027 on pfs_months is modified by other features. Specifically, after a systematic interaction screen testing feature_027 × every other feature in an OLS adjusting for all main effects, at least one statistically large modifier (|t|>20, p<1e-50) will emerge.

analyses.code: for each candidate tx in {feature_027, feature_011, feature_012, ..., feature_022}: for each other f: OLS pfs_months ~ all + tx*f; collect p_value of interaction term
analyses.result_summary: Strongest interactions for feature_027: feature_023 (coef=-1.63, p<1e-300), feature_006 (coef=-1.46, p<1e-300), feature_022 (coef=-0.99, p=3.8e-104), feature_005 (coef=-0.026/unit, p<1e-300), feature_011 (coef=+0.89, p=1.9e-57). All four variables (feature_006, feature_023, feature_022, feature_005) attenuate the feature_027 benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the subgroup feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0, the unadjusted effect of feature_027 on pfs_months is large and positive (>+2 months); outside that subgroup the effect is essentially zero.

analyses.code: Stratified means by joint (feature_006, feature_023, feature_022)
analyses.result_summary: 0 modifiers (n=17372): m0=4.12, m1=6.88, diff=+2.76 mo (p<1e-300). 1 modifier (n=24429): diff=+0.05 mo (NS). 2 modifiers (n=8199): diff=-0.04 mo (NS). Treatment effect collapses with even one of feature_006=1, feature_023=1, or feature_022=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Inside the subgroup feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0, the strongest residual treatment-effect modifier is feature_005 (continuous), and the relationship is monotonic: higher feature_005 reduces the feature_027 benefit.

analyses.code: Within sub: scan tx*f interactions for all features
analyses.result_summary: feature_005 emerges with the strongest interaction (coef=-0.064/unit, p≈0). Other modest interactions (feature_015 +0.006/unit, feature_001 -0.030/level, feature_014 -0.026) are 1-2 orders of magnitude weaker.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0, the modifying effect of feature_005 on the feature_027 benefit follows a sharp threshold near feature_005=5 (a clinically familiar normal-range cutoff): below the threshold the benefit is approximately +5 months; at or above it the benefit is approximately 0.

In the complete responder subgroup feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<5, the unadjusted effect of feature_027 on pfs_months is approximately +5 months and far exceeds the average treatment effect estimated on the full cohort.
Outside that 4-predicate responder subgroup, feature_027 has no meaningful effect on pfs_months (|effect|<0.1 mo).

analyses.result_summary: n=9326 (treated 1903, control 7423). m0=4.088, m1=9.101, diff=+5.013 mo, p<1e-300. Adjusted (OLS with all features) coefficient = +2.49 mo, p<1e-300 — the unadjusted gap is partly mediated by the prognostic background, but signed direction and overwhelming significance are confirmed.

analyses.code: out = complement of subgroup; ttest by feature_027
analyses.result_summary: n=40674. m0=4.123, m1=4.148, diff=+0.025 mo, p=0.33. No detectable feature_027 effect outside the responder subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: All four predicates of the responder subgroup are independently necessary: dropping any one of feature_006=0, feature_023=0, feature_022=0, or feature_005<5 admits patients in whom feature_027 has no effect.

analyses.code: Recompute feature_027 effect after relaxing each predicate one at a time
analyses.result_summary: Drop f006=0: among (f023=0,f022=0,f005<5) the diff drops to +2.6 mo because adding f006=1 patients dilutes signal (their diff is +0.07). Drop f023=0: similar dilution (their diff +0.07). Drop f022=0: the f022=1 contributes near-zero or slightly negative effect (-0.20 in this stratum). Drop f005<5: the f005>=5 patients contribute +0.03 mo. Each predicate is necessary; none is redundant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: No other binary feature in the dataset (feature_011, feature_016, feature_028, feature_002, feature_024, feature_010, feature_012, feature_018, feature_020, feature_031) shows treatment-like behavior, defined as a clean differential effect on pfs_months across some interpretable subgroup.

analyses.code: Within the clean prognostic subgroup (f006=0, f023=0, f022=0), means by each candidate binary
analyses.result_summary: All candidate binaries show |effect|<0.4 mo within the clean subgroup, none statistically large. Within the further-refined responder subgroup (also f005<5), no other binary shows interaction p<0.05 after Bonferroni adjustment for ~25 tests. feature_027 is the unique treatment-like indicator in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_015 acts as a strong, monotone, additive prognostic feature (each unit ≈ +0.18 mo of pfs_months in OLS) and not as a treatment-effect modifier — its tx_x_feature_015 interaction inside the responder subgroup is small (~+0.005/unit) and a small fraction of the main feature_015 effect.
feature_001 (3-level) acts mainly as a prognostic ordinal severity-like factor (-1.17 mo per level) and not as a treatment-effect modifier of feature_027 (interaction p=0.18 in stratified means).

analyses.code: OLS (in responder sub) pfs ~ all + tx*feature_015
analyses.result_summary: Main feature_015 coef +0.177/unit; interaction tx_x_f015 coef +0.0054/unit (p=1.6e-38). The interaction is statistically significant in the n=9326 sub but only ~3% the size of the main effect, so feature_015 is overwhelmingly prognostic, not predictive.

analyses.code: Stratified feature_027 effect by feature_001 levels (already done in iteration 6)
analyses.result_summary: Effect roughly constant (+0.93, +0.99, +0.90 across levels). Interaction term -0.04/level, p=0.18 — not significant after the 4-predicate subgroup is defined.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The final, best-supported treatment-effect heterogeneity hypothesis for outcome pfs_months in this cohort: feature_027 (the treatment indicator) prolongs pfs_months by roughly +5 months in patients defined by feature_006=0 AND feature_023=0 AND feature_022=0 AND feature_005<5, and has essentially no effect outside that subgroup.

analyses.code: Final consolidated test: ttest_ind inside vs outside the 4-predicate subgroup, plus interaction model with all three binary modifiers and feature_005<5 indicator
analyses.result_summary: Inside subgroup (n=9326): feature_027 raises pfs_months from 4.09 to 9.10 (+5.01 mo, p<1e-300). Outside subgroup (n=40674): feature_027 changes pfs_months from 4.12 to 4.15 (+0.025 mo, p=0.33). The contrast between in-subgroup and out-of-subgroup treatment effect is itself overwhelmingly significant (interaction p<1e-300). Direction is confirmed positive; the complete subgroup definition includes all four predicates, each independently necessary.
